description: The rigid, outermost mechanical layer of Earth, comprising the crust and the uppermost portion of the mantle. Broken into tectonic plates that move over the asthenosphere.

contents.name: averageThicknessKm
contents.description: Average thickness of the Lithosphere in kilometres (typically 100–200 km).

contents.name: numberOfTectonicPlates
contents.description: Number of major tectonic plates making up the Lithosphere.

contents.name: seismicActivity
contents.description: Current global seismic activity level (quiescent, minor, moderate, major, great).

contents.name: isPartOf
contents.description: The Lithosphere is part of Earth.

contents.name: interactsWith
contents.description: The Lithosphere interacts with the Biosphere through soil formation, mineral cycles, and tectonic events.

description: The outermost solid layer of the Lithosphere, comprising oceanic crust (5–10 km thick) and continental crust (30–50 km thick). The layer on which all surface life exists.

contents.name: crustType
contents.description: Type of crust: oceanic or continental.

contents.name: averageThicknessKm
contents.description: Average thickness of this crust type in kilometres.

description: The layer of Earth between the Crust and the Core, extending from approximately 35–2900 km depth. Composed of silicate rock and drives tectonic plate movement through convection.

contents.name: depthRangeKm
contents.description: Depth range of the Mantle, approximately 35–2900 km.

contents.name: avgTemperatureCelsius
contents.description: Average temperature within the Mantle, approximately 1000–3700°C.

contents.name: convectionActive
contents.description: Indicates whether mantle convection currents are currently active.

description: The innermost layer of Earth, comprising a liquid outer core (2900–5150 km depth) and a solid inner core (5150–6371 km depth). Composed primarily of iron and nickel. Generates Earth's magnetic field.

contents.name: coreType
contents.description: Whether this is the inner (solid) or outer (liquid) core.

contents.name: depthRangeKm
contents.description: Depth range of this core layer in kilometres.

contents.name: avgTemperatureCelsius
contents.description: Estimated temperature of the Core, approximately 5000–6000°C.

contents.name: generatesMagneticField
contents.description: Indicates that the liquid outer core generates Earth's geomagnetic field.
